title: hymenophyllum ivohibense
intro: Hymenophyllum ivohibense, also known as the Ivohibe fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Hymenophyllaceae. It is native to the island of Madagascar and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine. The plant has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. In terms of herbal preparations, Hymenophyllum ivohibense is typically consumed as a tea or infusion, although it is also used in some traditional remedies as a poultice or topical application.
